reason: Defines Snacks terminal API, required keymap semantics, terminal selector implementation, Windows shell migration, scoped keymaps, and Overseer strategy caveat.
Defines lazy.nvim plugin spec conventions, platform guard expectations, and Neovim headless verification requirements.
Defines plugin-owned keymap/UI organization and current terminal UI ownership.
Defines plugin UI option/keymap patterns, leader group conventions, and desc requirements.
Defines terminal-mode mapping scope requirements and avoiding broad TermOpen mappings that affect Claude Code terminals.
Defines UI/config checks for keymap conflicts, plugin specs, Windows/WSL/Claude terminal safety.
Defines runtime validation and optional plugin API access expectations.
Ensures new terminal helpers are minimal and do not duplicate existing logic unnecessarily.
